findings.description: Direct contradiction: Overview states 'All pipeline stages run in parallel' while Stage 2 states 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins'.
findings.evidence: Overview: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Stage 2: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'
findings.suggestion: Reconcile the execution model. Either remove the parallel claim from the Overview or document which stages are parallel and which are sequential with a dependency graph.

findings.description: Dangling reference: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' No Section 6 exists in this document.
findings.evidence: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' Document ends at Conclusions with no Section 6 present.
findings.suggestion: Either add a Section 6 covering security considerations or remove the reference and inline the relevant security information in Stage 4.

findings.description: Batch/streaming performance claim is factually inverted. Streaming is more efficient for small files; batch incurs setup overhead that yields no benefit at small sizes.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' Standard data engineering knowledge: streaming outperforms batch at small sizes due to batch setup overhead.
findings.suggestion: Correct the claim: streaming is generally faster for small files. If the benchmark result is genuine and unusual, explain the specific conditions that produced it (e.g., unusual network topology, specific framework overhead).

findings.description: 99.9% latency reduction claim is implausible and unsupported. No baseline, conditions, or methodology are provided.
findings.evidence: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.' No baseline, conditions, or methodology stated.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a defensible, sourced performance figure. If 99.9% is genuine, provide the baseline measurement, test conditions, and data source.

findings.description: Benchmark claim has no attribution — 'Benchmarks show' with no source, methodology, or conditions named.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' No source or methodology cited.
findings.suggestion: Cite the benchmark source and conditions, or replace with attributed data. Note: this finding is independent of C-003 — even if the direction were corrected, the unattributed claim would remain a traceability gap.

findings.description: Pipeline and workflow used interchangeably and inconsistently — the document never defines whether they are the same thing or distinct entities.
findings.evidence: Operational Notes: 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored.' Conclusions: 'a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' Treated as distinct in both instances but with different meanings.
findings.suggestion: Define pipeline and workflow clearly at first use (or in a Definitions section) and use them consistently throughout.

findings.description: 'Standard retry policy' is referenced but never defined anywhere in the document.
findings.evidence: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures.' No definition of retry count, backoff strategy, or timeout.
findings.suggestion: Define the retry policy (e.g., '3 retries with exponential backoff, max 30s timeout') or reference the configuration file/service that specifies it.

findings.description: Scope contradiction: Conclusions describe 'adding a real-time streaming workflow' as a future enhancement, but Stage 2 already describes the system as handling both batch and streaming processing modes.
findings.evidence: Conclusions: 'Future enhancements include adding a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' Stage 2 discusses both batch and streaming processing modes as current capability.
findings.suggestion: Reconcile: if streaming is already present, remove it from the future enhancements list. If it is genuinely future, remove streaming references from Stage 2 or mark them as planned.

findings.description: The 5-minute polling interval in Stage 4 contradicts the sub-second latency claim in Stage 3 — it is logically inconsistent to claim sub-second end-to-end latency while health checks only run every 5 minutes.
findings.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system polls health endpoints every 5 minutes.' Stage 3: 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9%' (implying sub-second delivery). A 5-minute polling interval cannot detect or respond to sub-second latency degradation.
findings.suggestion: Align the monitoring polling frequency with the latency SLO, or clarify that the 5-minute interval is for infrastructure health and a separate mechanism handles delivery SLA monitoring.

findings.description: 'Large files' are described as handled differently via an overflow queue, but the size threshold for 'large' is never defined.
findings.evidence: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.' No size threshold (e.g., >500MB, >1GB) specified.
findings.suggestion: Define the large file threshold explicitly (e.g., 'Files exceeding 500MB are routed to the overflow queue').

findings.description: ETL acronym not expanded on first use.
findings.evidence: 'an internal ETL system' — ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) not expanded.
findings.suggestion: Expand on first use: 'an internal ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) system'.

findings.description: Authentication type is unspecified — the document states authentication is used but names no mechanism.
findings.evidence: 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' No mechanism (API key, OAuth, mTLS, etc.) named.
findings.suggestion: Name the authentication mechanism in use, or reference the security documentation where it is specified.

findings.description: Stage 4 and Operational Notes repeat substantially the same information about the Prometheus dashboard.
findings.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system uses a Prometheus dashboard.' Operational Notes bullet 1: 'Monitored via the Prometheus dashboard.' Near-duplicate content across sections.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate monitoring details into Stage 4 and reference from Operational Notes, or remove the redundant mention.
